 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-7wh.4","title":"Implement history, show, and version CLI commands","description":"## Task
Implement the history browsing and detail viewing CLI commands.

## Commands

### slb history
Browse and search request history.

Flags:
- --query/-q: FTS search query
- --status: Filter by status (pending, approved, rejected, executed, etc.)
- --agent: Filter by agent name
- --tier: Filter by risk tier
- --since: Only show requests after date
- --limit: Max results (default 50)
- --format: Output format (table, json)

JSON output: Array of request summaries

### slb show <request-id>
Show detailed information about a specific request.

Flags:
- --with-reviews: Include full review details
- --with-execution: Include execution details
- --with-attachments: Include attachment content

JSON output: Full request object with nested data

### slb version
Show version and build information.

Flags:
- --json: Output as JSON
- --check: Check for updates (optional, future)

Output:
slb version X.Y.Z
  Built: 2025-12-13
  Commit: abc123
  Go: go1.25
  Config: ~/.slb/config.toml
  Project: .slb/state.db

JSON:
{
  "version": "X.Y.Z",
  "commit": "abc123",
  "build_date": "2025-12-13",
  "go_version": "go1.25",
  "config_paths": {...}
}

## Files
- internal/cli/history.go
- internal/cli/show.go
- internal/cli/version.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] history search with FTS works
- [ ] show displays full details
- [ ] version shows build info
- [ ] JSON output for all commands
- [ ] Filters work correctly","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:33:22.678792898-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T20:20:07.539157277-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T20:20:07.539157277-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-7wh.4","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-3s8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:33:22.679741856-06:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-7wh.4","depends_on_id":"simultaneous_launch_button-7wh.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:33:22.680615653-06:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-7y5","title":"Testing Strategy: SLB Comprehensive Test Suite (Epic)","description":"## Executive Summary

This epic encompasses all testing efforts for the SLB (Simultaneous Launch Button) project. The goal is to achieve 90%+ unit test coverage across all packages and comprehensive E2E integration tests that validate the full two-person rule workflow.

## Current State (As of December 2025)

### Coverage Analysis
| Package | Current | Target | Gap |
|---------|---------|--------|-----|
| core | 51.3% | 90% | 38.7% |
| daemon | 53.6% | 90% | 36.4% |
| db | 61.7% | 90% | 28.3% |
| cli | 0% | 90% | 90% |
| config | 0% | 90% | 90% |
| git | 0% | 90% | 90% |
| integrations | 0% | 90% | 90% |
| output | 0% | 90% | 90% |
| tui | 0% | 90% | 90% |
| utils | 0% | 90% | 90% |

### Test Philosophy
- **No Mocks**: Use real SQLite databases for testing
- **Table-Driven**: Prefer table-driven tests for clarity
- **Deterministic**: Tests must not be flaky
- **Fast**: Total test suite <30 seconds
- **Isolated**: Each test creates own environment

## Dependency Structure

```
Test Infrastructure (simultaneous_launch_button-v2x)
    │
    ├── Unit Tests: 0% Packages (6 beads)
    │   ├── CLI (simultaneous_launch_button-i8b)
    │   ├── Config (simultaneous_launch_button-3z3)
    │   ├── Git (simultaneous_launch_button-hxb)
    │   ├── Integrations (simultaneous_launch_button-4hy)
    │   ├── Output (simultaneous_launch_button-6ac)
    │   ├── TUI (simultaneous_launch_button-eik)
    │   └── Utils (simultaneous_launch_button-l0f)
    │
    ├── Unit Tests: Improve Existing (3 beads)
    │   ├── Core 51%→90% (simultaneous_launch_button-6zj)
    │   ├── Daemon 54%→90% (simultaneous_launch_button-wr8)
    │   └── DB 62%→90% (simultaneous_launch_button-djg)
    │
    ├── E2E Tests (epic + 4 scenarios) (simultaneous_launch_button-6fw)
    │   ├── Multi-Agent Approval (simultaneous_launch_button-6fw.1)
    │   ├── Risk Tier Classification (simultaneous_launch_button-6fw.2)
    │   ├── Session Timeout (simultaneous_launch_button-6fw.3)
    │   └── Git Rollback (simultaneous_launch_button-6fw.4)
    │
    └── CI Integration (simultaneous_launch_button-jl8)
```

## Implementation Order

### Phase 1: Foundation (Week 1)
1. simultaneous_launch_button-v2x: Test Infrastructure
2. simultaneous_launch_button-l0f: Utils tests (minimal dependencies)

### Phase 2: Core Coverage (Week 2)
3. simultaneous_launch_button-djg: DB tests (most used, highest existing coverage)
4. simultaneous_launch_button-6zj: Core tests (business logic)
5. simultaneous_launch_button-3z3: Config tests

### Phase 3: Interface Layers (Week 3)
6. simultaneous_launch_button-6ac: Output tests
7. simultaneous_launch_button-hxb: Git tests  
8. simultaneous_launch_button-i8b: CLI tests

### Phase 4: Advanced Features (Week 4)
9. simultaneous_launch_button-wr8: Daemon tests
10. simultaneous_launch_button-4hy: Integrations tests
11. simultaneous_launch_button-eik: TUI tests

### Phase 5: Integration (Week 5)
12. simultaneous_launch_button-6fw: E2E test suite
13. simultaneous_launch_button-jl8: CI integration

## Success Metrics

### Quantitative
- Overall coverage: 90%+
- E2E scenarios: 10+ passing
- CI pipeline: <5 minute runtime
- Zero flaky tests

### Qualitative
- Tests are self-documenting
- Failures are easy to debug
- New developers can understand codebase via tests
- Refactoring is safe with test coverage

## Risk Mitigation

### Risk: Tests Slow Down Development
Mitigation: Keep individual tests fast (<100ms), parallelize where possible

### Risk: Flaky Tests
Mitigation: No time-dependent tests, deterministic randomness, isolated environments

### Risk: Coverage Gaming
Mitigation: Focus on meaningful assertions, code review for test quality

## Related Beads

All testing beads are children of this epic:
- Test Infrastructure: simultaneous_launch_button-v2x
- Unit Test Beads: simultaneous_launch_button-i8b, simultaneous_launch_button-3z3, simultaneous_launch_button-6ac, simultaneous_launch_button-hxb, simultaneous_launch_button-4hy, simultaneous_launch_button-eik, simultaneous_launch_button-l0f, simultaneous_launch_button-6zj, simultaneous_launch_button-wr8, simultaneous_launch_button-djg
- E2E Epic: simultaneous_launch_button-6fw (with children .1-.4)
- CI Integration: simultaneous_launch_button-jl8","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-13T21:04:39.119700896-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T21:04:39.119700896-06:00","labels":["epic","priority-high","testing"]}
 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-87z","title":"P5.4: Run Command Execution Tests","description":"## Target Functions
- runApprovedRequest (0%)
- createRunLogFile (0%)
- writeError (0%)

## Approach
These involve process execution which requires:
1. Either test commands (echo, true, false)
2. Or mock interfaces (if we want pure unit tests)

## Recommended Strategy
Use test commands that are safe:
- 'echo test' for success path
- 'false' for failure path
- 'exit 1' for error handling

## Tests Required
- TestRunApprovedRequest_Success (with 'echo test')
- TestRunApprovedRequest_Failure (with 'false')
- TestCreateRunLogFile_CreatesFile
- TestWriteError_WritesToStderr

## Acceptance
- 80%+ coverage using safe test commands
- Document limitation: cannot test arbitrary commands

 {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-8td","title":"P2.2: Extract autoApproveCaution Decision Logic","description":"## Target Function
`autoApproveCaution` (watch.go:223) - Currently 0% coverage

## PRIORITY: CRITICAL
This function contains SAFETY-CRITICAL decision logic that determines
whether a command can be auto-approved. This logic MUST be tested.

## The Problem
autoApproveCaution decides whether to auto-approve "caution" tier commands.
Currently untestable because it's embedded in the watch polling loop.

## The Solution: Extract shouldAutoApproveCaution

### Before
```go
func autoApproveCaution(...) error {
    // Safety decision logic mixed with DB calls
    if config.AutoApproveCaution && req.RiskTier == Caution {
        // complex conditions
        db.AutoApprove(req)
    }
}
```

### After
```go
// Pure function - MUST have 100% test coverage
func shouldAutoApproveCaution(req *Request, config *AutoApproveConfig) (bool, string) {
    if !config.AutoApproveCaution {
        return false, "auto-approve disabled"
    }
    if req.RiskTier != RiskTierCaution {
        return false, "not caution tier"
    }
    if req.MinApprovals > 0 && !config.BypassMinApprovals {
        return false, "requires explicit approval"
    }
    return true, "caution tier auto-approved"
}

// Wrapper - acceptable at 0%
func autoApproveCaution(...) error {
    ok, reason := shouldAutoApproveCaution(req, config)
    log.Debug("auto-approve decision", "ok", ok, "reason", reason)
    if ok {
        db.AutoApprove(req)
    }
}
```

## Tests to Write (COMPREHENSIVE)

```go
func TestShouldAutoApproveCaution_DisabledInConfig(t *testing.T) {...}
func TestShouldAutoApproveCaution_WrongRiskTier(t *testing.T) {
    // Verify Dangerous and Safe tiers are never auto-approved
}
func TestShouldAutoApproveCaution_RequiresMinApprovals(t *testing.T) {...}
func TestShouldAutoApproveCaution_BypassMinApprovals(t *testing.T) {...}
func TestShouldAutoApproveCaution_HappyPath(t *testing.T) {...}
```

## Why This Is P0
- Auto-approval bypass could lead to dangerous commands being executed
- This is the "two-person rule" safety feature
- Bugs here = security vulnerabilities

## Success Criteria
- shouldAutoApproveCaution has 100% coverage
- Every decision branch tested
- Negative cases tested (should NOT approve)
- Edge cases documented

## Estimated Effort: Medium (3-4 hours) due to criticality","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T10:29:26.534344368-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T11:53:59.646697992-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T11:53:59.646697992-06:00","close_reason":"Extracted shouldAutoApproveCaution pure function with 100% test coverage.  {"id":"simultaneous_launch_button-9c0","title":"Phase 1: Partial Coverage → 100% (Easy Wins)","description":"## Purpose & Rationale

This phase targets 14 functions currently at 71-95% coverage. These represent the lowest-hanging fruit because:
1. Test infrastructure already exists for these functions
2. We just need edge cases and error paths
3. Each function needs only 1-3 additional tests to reach 100%
4. Combined, this phase could raise overall coverage by ~5-8%

## Functions to Complete (by file)

### daemon.go
- `daemonLogPath` (75.0%) - needs empty path test
- `daemonProjectStats` (90.0%) - needs error handling test
- `tailFileLines` (94.7%) - needs boundary conditions

### help.go  
- `detectWidth` (83.3%) - needs non-terminal environment test
- `gradientText` (90.9%) - needs edge case for very short strings

### history.go
- `listRequestsWithFilters` (81.8%) - needs empty result and error path tests

### review.go
- `showRequestDetails` (73.6%) - needs multiple review scenarios

### root.go
- `GetDB` (71.4%) - needs all fallback paths tested
- `GetActor` (92.3%) - needs hostname lookup failure

### session.go
- `projectPath` (83.3%) - needs git root fallback test

### approve.go
- `buildAgentMailNotifier` (83.3%) - needs enabled config path

### completion.go
- `completeSessionIDs` (90.5%) - needs database error test

### attachments.go
- `CollectAttachments` (88.9%) - needs error path for file operations

### patterns.go
- `outputPatterns` (95.5%) - needs empty patterns test

## Success Criteria
- All 14 functions reach 100% coverage
- Tests are isolated (no test pollution)
- Each test documents the edge case it covers

## Estimated Impact
57.7% → ~63-65% overall coverage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T10:26:06.950988476-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T11:50:41.736525202-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T11:50:41.736525202-06:00","close_reason":"All 6 P1.x child beads completed. CLI coverage improved from 57.7% to 58.9%, with all targeted functions reaching 95-100% coverage.
